entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: Glycine max L. Merr., the primary crop studied for diversity and core collection development.

entities.name: CORE COLLECTIONS
entities.description: Smaller germplasm panels that represent the diversity of larger collections.

entities.name: WORLD VEGETABLE CENTER
entities.description: The institution in Taiwan that held the soybean accessions and historical data.

entities.name: TAIWAN
entities.description: The country where the World Vegetable Center is located.

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: The characteristic used to split the soybean collection into large-seeded and small-seeded groups.

entities.name: 100 SEED WEIGHT
entities.description: The specific metric used to define seed size, with a threshold of 25g.

entities.name: VEGETABLE SOYBEAN
entities.description: Large-seeded soybean, also known as edamame.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE TRAITS
entities.description: Seven measurable traits used to develop the core collections.

entities.name: QUALITATIVE TRAITS
entities.description: Fourteen descriptive traits used to develop the core collections.

entities.name: AUTUMN SEASON
entities.description: The specific time of year when the quantitative and qualitative traits were collected.
